name: Blackberry Cobbler Macguire
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. In a 10-inch pie dish or baking dish, melt half of the butter and keep the remaining half chilled.
3. Heat water and 1 cup sugar in a saucepan until the sugar is dissolved.
4. Pulse flour, baking powder, and the remaining chilled butter into small pieces in a food processor until the mixture resembles coarse meal.
5. Add milk to the food processor and pulse just until dough forms.
6. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and roll it out to about an 11 by 9-inch rectangle.
7. Sprinkle cinnamon over the dough, then evenly sprinkle blackberries on top.
8. Starting with a long side, carefully roll up the dough in a jelly-roll fashion and cut into 1-1/2 inch slices (the slices will be messy).
9. Arrange the slices, cut-side up, on top of the butter in the baking dish; pour sugar syrup over the slices, soaking the dough.
10. Bake in the middle of the oven for 45 minutes.
11. Sprinkle the remaining 2 tablespoons of sugar over the top and bake an additional 15 minutes or until nicely browned.
Note: Other fruit can be substituted for blackberries.
